prompt:
You are working on the TaskFlow Pro project at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-3mJh4T.

Review the notification system implementation in this codebase. Two previous developers may have made **conflicting architectural choices** about how services communicate for notifications.

Your task:
1. Identify any architectural conflicts in the notification approach
2. Evaluate both approaches (event-driven vs direct calls) in the context of this project
3. Choose the better approach with clear justification
4. Unify the codebase so it follows a single consistent notification architecture
5. Ensure all tests pass after unification
6. Document your decision and rationale (in code comments or coordination files)

parameters.task: Review and unify conflicting notification system architectures (event-driven vs direct calls)

parameters.prompt:
Explore the notification system in this codebase thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. All files related to notifications (services, controllers, models, tests, events, etc.)
2. How notifications are triggered - look for both event-driven patterns (event emitters, pub/sub, message buses) and direct service calls
3. Any architectural conflicts between different approaches to notification delivery
4. The test files for notifications

Search broadly - check src/, lib/, services/, tests/, etc. Report file paths, key code patterns, and any conflicts you find. Be very thorough - read the actual implementation files.

parameters.description: Run existing tests to see current state

alternatives.option: Keep direct calls, convert UserService to also use direct NotificationService injection
alternatives.reason_rejected: Contradicts the project's stated architecture, loses EventBus infrastructure benefits (event logging, async, typed events), and would require adding NotificationService as a dependency to UserService
Inconsistency makes the codebase harder to maintain and reason about, confuses new developers about the intended pattern

parameters.context: Two prior developers made contradictory decisions: one unified on EventBus, another switched to direct calls. The codebase currently has OrderService using direct NotificationService calls while UserService uses EventBus pub/sub. CallbackRegistry exists as unused dead code. The project's own index.ts declares 'Event-driven notifications' as a key architectural decision.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is the better choice because: (1) it's already well-built infrastructure with typed events, async handlers, event logging, and unsubscribe semantics; (2) the project's architecture docs in index.ts explicitly declare event-driven notifications as the intended pattern; (3) loose coupling means services don't need to know about NotificationService; (4) UserService already uses EventBus correctly; (5) event types for orders are already defined but unused. Direct calls create tight coupling and inconsistency with the rest of the codebase.
parameters.summary: Unify all service communication on EventBus pattern, removing direct NotificationService injection from OrderService

alternatives.option: Keep direct calls, convert UserService to also use direct injection
alternatives.reason_rejected: Contradicts project's stated architecture, loses EventBus benefits (event logging, async, typed events)
Inconsistency makes codebase harder to maintain and confuses developers about intended pattern

parameters.context: Two prior developers made contradictory decisions: one unified on EventBus, another switched to direct calls. The codebase has OrderService using direct NotificationService calls while UserService uses EventBus pub/sub. CallbackRegistry is dead code. The project's index.ts declares event-driven notifications as the intended architecture.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is the better choice: (1) well-built infrastructure with typed events, async handlers, event logging, unsubscribe semantics; (2) project docs declare event-driven as intended pattern; (3) loose coupling - services don't need to know about NotificationService; (4) UserService already uses EventBus correctly; (5) order event types are already defined but unused.
parameters.summary: Unify all service communication on EventBus pattern, removing direct NotificationService injection from OrderService

parameters.detail: Changes made: (1) OrderService now takes EventBus instead of NotificationService, emits order:created and order:status-changed events; (2) NotificationService gains subscribeToEvents(eventBus) method to listen for order events; (3) Deleted dead CallbackRegistry code; (4) Updated all tests to wire EventBus between services. Both OrderService and UserService now consistently use EventBus for inter-service communication.
parameters.summary: Unified notification architecture on EventBus: OrderService emits events, NotificationService subscribes. All 74 tests pass.
